title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection · Type-System Trace: Broker Fit Boundary
summary: A request path adds a broker without a latency or failure reason, hides synchronous coupling behind RPC messages, and uses one global queue for work distribution, broadcasting, and replay. Planned artifact: Add and verify the broker fit boundary increment in pattern selection and coupling decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.
Choose choreography or orchestration by workflow visibility, policy ownership, compensation, evolution, and failure diagnosis needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Broker Fit Boundary · Theory Broker Fit Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6283 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.
